title: 871 Civilians Killed, including 179 Children, in Eastern Ghouta in a Month after Security Council Resolution 2401 on Cessation of Hostilities was Adopted
strengths: Provides a clean one-month civilian total and child-death count for Eastern Ghouta.
limitations: Retained text used here does not clearly assign the full monthly total to a single inflicting actor within the same sentence, so episode-total inflicting-side remains null.
notes: Retained wording: 871 civilians killed, including 179 children, in Eastern Ghouta in the month after UNSC Resolution 2401.

title: As the World Looks Away, More Than 250 People Have Been Killed in Syria's Eastern Ghouta
pages_or_sections: Population context and short-window escalation summary
strengths: Provides a contemporaneous trapped-population estimate and qualitative attribution to Syrian government intensification.
limitations: The main retained numeric death figure from this article is a two-day spike before the episode window selected here, so it is used only for context rather than as an episode estimate.
notes: Retained wording used here: around 400,000 civilians remain trapped in Eastern Ghouta.

title: As Syria's Death Toll Increases, The U.N. Is Decrying 'One of the Worst Humanitarian Catastrophes' of the 21st Century
pages_or_sections: Civilian death total since late April 2019 and trapped-population context
strengths: Provides a dated civilian death total and explicitly frames the offensive as a Syrian-government campaign supported by Russia.
limitations: The 100 civilians / 26 children in 10 days are sub-window figures rather than the full episode total.
notes: Retained wording: at least 450 civilians had died since late April; the offensive was by the Syrian government, supported by Russia; Idlib is home to about 3 million trapped civilians.

title: Targeting Life in Idlib: Syrian and Russian Strikes on Civilian Infrastructure
pages_or_sections: Opening summary on 11-month offensive and harm to civilians
strengths: Qualitative support that Syrian-Russian attacks harmed civilians and civilian infrastructure.
limitations: Used qualitatively here; not the numeric basis for the retained 2019 episode estimates.
notes: Helps support the offensive-actor framing in the Idlib row.
